title: Resource Graphs, Cache Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Non-Claims · Contract Triage
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in static resource and delivery evidence dossier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.
Separate wire, decoded, retained, intrinsic-media, rendered, and duplicate-transfer sizes with missing evidence visible.
Report cache fields, validators, variation, redirects, compression, and cold or warm assumptions without simulating browser cache blindly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Resource Reference Graph
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 8242 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.

Inspect the Resource Reference Graph trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Resource Reference Graph.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope. For Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Resource Reference Graph, keep Resource Reference Graph connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: For Resource Reference Graph, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

The Resource Reference Graph record says static resource and delivery evidence dossier looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Resource Reference Graph.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Performance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-performance-nonclaim-1254i0" that uses case 1986 to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Performance Nonclaim.
